response.justification: Maria Rodriguez has a broad and impressive web/full-stack engineering background, but she falls critically short of the minimum qualifications for this role. The position requires 8 years of C++ experience as a hard floor — her resume shows approximately 6 months of C/C++ work at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a brief mention in a thesis project, with C++ absent entirely from her listed skills. There is no evidence of sustained cloud compute platform ownership, infrastructure leadership at the required scale, or technical leadership of cloud/DevOps teams. The bulk of her experience is in TypeScript/Node.js web and automation platforms, which does not map to the demands of an internal cloud compute platform lead.
strengths: Demonstrated experience with high-throughput distributed systems at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O and low-latency techniques
Strong software craftsmanship culture (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) and a track record of mentoring through workshops and code katas
Staff-level engineering roles with cross-team contributions and evidence of owning ambiguous projects end-to-end (GISCollective, AI-agent infrastructure at re:cinq)
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months) versus the 8-year minimum — the single most critical disqualifier for this role
No cloud compute platform or infrastructure leadership experience; AWS CDK usage at Optilyz for a direct-mail pricing service does not constitute cloud architecture at scale
No evidence of technical leadership scoped to cloud or DevOps engineering teams; staff roles were individual-contributor-leaning without clear people or technical strategy ownership
